The evidence cited across the provided context consistently states: “The backup retention period is exactly seven years according to the cited document.” However, no actual excerpt, citation, or verifiable reference to the underlying “cited document” is provided in any of the fragments. All three sources—partner bulletin, FAQ answer, and secondary sources—repeat this claim verbatim, and the FAQ explicitly labels it a “copied low-confidence claim.” There is no independent, primary-source evidence (e.g., policy text, regulatory language, or official documentation) presented in the context to substantiate the seven-year retention period.

Therefore, the *only* evidence given is a repeated, uncited, low-confidence assertion—not authoritative or verified evidence.
